---
name: simplecontext
description: Provide lightweight, static repository context for the example project code-named BigDog.
---

# BigDog context skill

Use this skill when the user needs a small amount of stable domain context that should live directly in the repository.

## Why this skill exists

This is a deliberately tiny skill for workshop demos. It shows that a skill can be nothing more than reusable local knowledge when that is enough.

## Available context

- Project codename: BigDog
- Owner: Michael Coder
- Inventory number: 54321

## When to use a skill vs MCP

- Use this skill for fixed context that belongs in the repo.
- Use MCP when the user needs current status, remote records, or data from an external system.

## Example prompts

- "What stable project context should an agent know before drafting a demo?"
- "Show a simple skill that carries local domain knowledge without an external dependency."
